result:
Everything imports cleanly. Here's a summary of what was set up:

---

## Project Setup Complete Γ£ו

### Files created:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `requirements.txt` | Pinned dependencies: `plaid-python==38.4.0`, `flask==3.1.0` |
| `app.py` | Flask app with Plaid Link integration |

### Endpoints:

| Route | Method | Description |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| `/api/create-link-token` | POST | Creates a Link token to initialize Plaid Link in your frontend |
| `/api/exchange-token` | POST | Exchanges the public token from Link for a permanent access token |
| `/api/webhook` | POST | Handles Plaid webhook events (transactions, item errors) |

### Γתá∩╕ן Important note about the chub docs

The docs recommended installing `plaid-link-verify` Γאפ **this package does not exist on PyPI**. I omitted it entirely. Plaid's own SDK and [webhook verification docs](https://plaid.com/docs/api/webhooks/webhook-verification/) provide the official way to verify webhooks using JWT validation, which doesn't require any third-party package.

### To run:

```bash
export PLAID_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id
export PLAID_SECRET=your_sandbox_secret
python3 app.py
```

The server will start on `http://localhost:5000` using Plaid's Sandbox environment by default. Set `PLAID_ENV=production` when you're ready to go live.
